title: Incremental deployment — lab already has 1 scanner, adding 2 more
description: Lab already has 1 scanner purchased in 2022 that has been running for 3 years, and is now adding 2 more scanners. edge_cases.md flags incremental-vs-greenfield as the FIRST question Claude review should ask, but there is no wizard affordance for it. Expected outcome: the calculator treats this as a fresh greenfield deployment, double-counting capex and ramp against a baseline that already has partial productivity gains.

expected.notes: EXPECTED OUTCOME: calculator runs and produces a fresh-deployment NPV. The finding is that if you were honest about the existing scanner, the incremental NPV should SUBTRACT the baseline scenario (what if the 1 existing scanner already delivered 30% of the productivity ramp for those 3 years). Outcome: wizard affordance for 'existing digital baseline' + a subtract-baseline calculation mode. This is flagged in edge_cases.md as 'should be first Claude review question'.
